questions.q: In which Indian city is the Qutub Minar located?
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar stands in the Mehrauli area of Delhi.

questions.q: What type of structure is the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar is a tall minaret, a soaring victory tower.

questions.q: Roughly how tall is the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar rises about 73 metres, making it one of the tallest brick minarets in the world.

questions.q: Who began the construction of the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din Aibak began building the Qutub Minar in 1199.

questions.q: The Qutub Minar is widely known by which nickname?
questions.explanation: It is celebrated as 'The Tower of Victory,' marking the founding of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: The minaret is built mainly of red and buff sandstone, with marble in upper portions.

questions.q: The Qutub Minar marks the founding of which empire in India?
questions.explanation: It was raised to commemorate the founding of the Delhi Sultanate around the turn of the 13th century.

questions.q: What is the international heritage status of the Qutub Minar complex?
options: A Wonder of the Ancient World
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar and its monuments were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1993.

questions.q: In which century did construction of the Qutub Minar begin?
questions.explanation: Work began in 1199 CE, at the turn of the 12th to 13th century.

questions.q: What is the general shape of the Qutub Minar as it rises?
options: A perfect cylinder of equal width
A tower that tapers as it rises
questions.explanation: The minaret tapers from a broad base to a narrow top across its storeys.

questions.q: How many storeys (stages) make up the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar is built in five distinct storeys, each marked by a projecting balcony.

questions.q: Which ruler completed the Qutub Minar after Qutb-ud-din Aibak?
questions.explanation: Aibak's successor and son-in-law Iltutmish completed the upper storeys of the minar.

questions.q: The mosque built beside the Qutub Minar is known by what name?
questions.explanation: The adjoining Quwwat-ul-Islam ('Might of Islam') Mosque is among the earliest mosques in India.

questions.q: What ancient metal object famous for not rusting stands within the Qutub complex?
questions.explanation: The rust-resistant Iron Pillar, dating to the Gupta era, stands in the Quwwat-ul-Islam courtyard.

questions.q: Which feature decorates the surface of the Qutub Minar's lower storeys?
options: Alternating fluted angular and rounded ribs with carved bands
questions.explanation: The lower storeys carry distinctive fluting with alternating angular and rounded ribs and inscribed bands.

questions.q: What kind of inscriptions cover the bands of the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: Ornamental bands bear Arabic Quranic verses and historical inscriptions in Kufic and Naskh scripts.

questions.q: Which architectural style does the Qutub Minar exemplify?
questions.explanation: It is a landmark example of early Indo-Islamic architecture combining Islamic forms with Indian craftsmanship.

questions.q: What is the unfinished tower nearby, intended to be twice the height of the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: The Alai Minar, begun by Alauddin Khalji, was abandoned after its first storey and remains a stump.

questions.q: Who built the ornate Alai Darwaza gateway in the Qutub complex?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji added the Alai Darwaza in 1311 as a southern gateway to the mosque.

questions.q: Which natural event has historically damaged the Qutub Minar, prompting repairs?
questions.explanation: Lightning and earthquakes damaged the top over the centuries, leading to repairs by later rulers.

questions.q: Approximately how many steps lead up the spiral staircase inside the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: A spiral staircase of about 379 steps climbs the interior of the minaret.

questions.q: Public access to the top of the Qutub Minar was permanently closed after which type of incident?
options: A stampede during a power failure
questions.explanation: After a 1981 stampede in the dark stairwell killed many visitors, interior access was closed to the public.

questions.q: After whom is the Qutub Minar most commonly said to be named?
options: Founder Qutb-ud-din Aibak (and the saint Qutbuddin Bakhtiar Kaki)
questions.explanation: The name is linked to its founder Qutb-ud-din Aibak and the revered Sufi saint Qutbuddin Bakhtiar Kaki.

questions.q: The Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque was famously constructed using materials from what earlier structures?
questions.explanation: The mosque was built reusing carved columns and stone from numerous dismantled Hindu and Jain temples on the site.

questions.q: Which ruler is credited with rebuilding the top storeys after lightning damage, adding a marble cupola in the 14th century?
questions.explanation: Firoz Shah Tughlaq repaired the upper storeys after lightning damage in 1368 and added marble facing.

questions.q: Who added a small cupola atop the minar in 1828 that was later removed and now sits in the garden, nicknamed 'Smith's Folly'?
questions.explanation: British engineer Major Robert Smith placed a Bengali-style cupola in 1828; it was removed and is now called Smith's Folly.

questions.q: Roughly what is the diameter of the Qutub Minar at its base?
questions.explanation: The base diameter is about 14.3 metres, tapering to roughly 2.7 metres at the top.

questions.q: The upper two storeys of the Qutub Minar are faced largely with which material, contrasting the lower sandstone?
questions.explanation: The upper storeys, rebuilt by Firoz Shah Tughlaq, use marble together with sandstone.

questions.q: Which Sufi saint's association is reflected in the popular naming of the Qutub Minar?
questions.explanation: Many accounts connect the tower's name to the Chishti Sufi saint Khwaja Qutbuddin Bakhtiar Kaki of Mehrauli.

questions.q: In what year was the Qutub Minar complex inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List?
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar and its monuments were added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1993.
